titleStmt.title: Letter from Francis Henry Downing to Matthew Nathan, 13 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Francis Henry Downing

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Francis Henry Downing (1865-1931), a solicitor from Kerry to the Under-Secretary Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). The letter acknowledges Nathans previous letter regarding amending the Tralee and Dingle Management Order 1896.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6648

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
22 Denny Street, Tralee 13th April, 1916 The Under-Secretary, Dublin Castle. 4339 Dear Sir, In reply to your letter of the 6th Instant enclosing draft Order
amending the Tralee and Dingle Committee of Management Order 1896, I beg to
say that I will furnish you with the observations of the County Council
thereon in the course of a few days. Yours faithfully, F H Downing
